events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess scenario: anchor bolts field-bent to fit on commercial structural steel column/beam erection. Need estimate measurable condition and state why out of tolerance. Need apply AISC 303 and OSHA 1926 Subpart R. Anchor bolts field-bent to fit is generally prohibited? AISC 303 Code of Standard Practice for Steel Buildings and Bridges: Section 7? Anchor rods: bending of anchor rods in the field to align with base plates is generally not permitted unless approved by engineer. AISC 303-16? It says anchor rods shall not be field-bent unless approved by the Engineer of Record. Field bending can cause cracking, reduced capacity, fatigue. OSHA 1926 Subpart R: steel erection, anchor bolts: columns must be anchored by a minimum of 4 anchor rods (1926.755(a)); field modification of anchor rods? OSHA prohibits bending anchor bolts? It requires that anchor bolts (anchor rods) be designed and installed per requirements; field bending may violate. So decision fail, risk high/critical? Non-compliant, installed-defective. Need value numeric reading or computed quantity. We can estimate e.g., anchor bolt bent offset 1.5 inches or bend angle 15 degrees. Need state why out of tolerance. AISC 303 tolerance for anchor rod placement maybe ±1/4 in? But field-bent is not a placement tolerance issue; it's a prohibited practice. Could estimate bend angle 20° exceeds allowable 0° (no field bending without EOR approval). Value maybe 20 degrees. Confidence 0.85. percent_complete rough-complete maybe 75. component_conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. findings: anchor bolts were field-bent to align with base plate holes; field bending not permitted without EOR approval and may crack/cause stress concentrations reducing tensile/shear capacity. actions: stop work on affected columns, notify EOR/inspector, replace bent anchor rods, verify base plate hole alignment and anchor rod layout before re-erection. references: AISC 303, OSHA 1926.755. Need JSON only. Ensure valid JSON.

    "Condition violates AISC 303 and OSHA 1926 Subpart R anchorage requirements."
  ],

    "Stop erection on affected columns.",
    "Notify the Engineer of Record and third-party inspector.",
    "Remove and replace field-bent anchor rods with new rods installed to approved layout.",
    "Verify base-plate hole pattern and anchor-rod placement before re-erection."
  ],
  "rationale": "Field-bent anchor bolts are a prohibited modification under AISC 303 unless explicitly approved by the Engineer of Record. The observed bend (estimated ~20°) indicates the rods were forced to fit rather than installed to design tolerances, creating a high-risk structural defect that compromises anchorage capacity and erection safety under OSHA 1926 Subpart R.",
  "references": [
    "AISC 303 - Code of Standard Practice for Steel Buildings and Bridges",
    "OSHA 1926 Subpart R - Steel Erection, 1926.755 Column Anchorage"
  ]
}
